---
title: 'Sheep'
date: '2010-04-11T14:45:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1944968468123779047'
blogger_filename: '/2010/04/sheep.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
---

<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/domestic-sheep-9abf50bc4bd3.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/domestic-sheep-a0da3c9464c0.jpg" /></a></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: x-large;">What about sheep? We are called to be sheep of the shepherd... Psalms 23 paints us as sheep. David the man after God's own heart talks of us as sheep... Are sheep Sheepish?</span><br />
<br />
<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 19px;">From <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domestic_sheep">Wikipedia</a>, the free encyclopedia</span><br />
<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">&nbsp;Use of the word&nbsp;<i>sheep</i>&nbsp;began in&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Middle_English" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Middle English">Middle English</a>&nbsp;as a derivation of the&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Old_English" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Old English">Old English</a>&nbsp;word&nbsp;<i>scÄ“ap</i>; it is both the singular and plural name for the animal.</span><br />
<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><br />
</span><br />
<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"></span><br />
<h2 style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; border-bottom-color: rgb(170, 170, 170); border-bottom-style: solid; border-bottom-width: 1px; color: black; font-size: 19px; font-weight: normal; margin-bottom: 0.6em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0.17em; padding-top: 0.5em;"><span class="mw-headline" id="Behavior_and_intelligence">Behavior and intelligence</span></h2><div><span class="mw-headline" id="Behavior_and_intelligence"><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;">Sheep are&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Predation" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Predation">prey</a>&nbsp;animals with a strong gregarious instinct, and a majority of sheep behaviors can be understood in these terms. The&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Dominance_hierarchy" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Dominance hierarchy">dominance hierarchy</a>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<i>Ovis aries</i>&nbsp;and its natural inclination to follow a leader to new pastures were the pivotal factors in it being one of the first domesticated livestock species.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-wild_33-0" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-wild-33" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>34<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;<b>All sheep have a tendency to congregate close to other members of a flock, although this behavior varies with breed.</b><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-smith5_11-1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-smith5-11" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>12<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Farmers exploit this behavior to keep sheep together on unfenced pastures and to move them more easily. Shepherds may also use&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Herding_dog" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Herding dog">Herding dogs</a>&nbsp;in this effort, whose highly bred&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Herding" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Herding">herding</a>&nbsp;ability can assist in moving flocks. <b>Sheep are also extremely food-oriented, and association of humans with regular feeding often results in sheep soliciting people for food.</b><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-34" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-34" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>35<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Those who are moving sheep may exploit this behavior by leading sheep with buckets of feed, rather than forcing their movements with herding.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-35" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-35" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>36<span>]</span></a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-36" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-36" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>37<span>]</span></a></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;">In regions where sheep have no natural predators, none of the native breeds of sheep exhibit a strong flocking behavior.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-begin_10-8" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-begin-10" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>11<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;<b>Sheep can also become&nbsp;</b><a class="mw-redirect" href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Hefted" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Hefted"><b>hefted</b></a><b>&nbsp;to one particular local pasture (heft) so they do not roam freely in unfenced landscapes</b>. Ewes teach the heft to their lambs, and if whole flocks are culled it must be retaught to the replacement animals.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-storey_6-5" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-storey-6" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>7<span>]</span></a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-37" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-37" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>38<span>]</span></a></sup></div><div class="thumb tleft" style="background-color: white; border-bottom-style: none; border-color: initial; border-color: initial; border-left-style: none; border-right-style: none; border-top-style: none; border-width: initial; border-width: initial; clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 0.8em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 1.4em; margin-top: 0.5em; width: auto;"><div class="thumbinner" style="background-color: #f9f9f9; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: solid; border-bottom-width: 1px; border-left-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-left-style: solid; border-left-width: 1px; border-right-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-right-style: solid; border-right-width: 1px; border-top-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-top-style: solid; border-top-width: 1px; font-size: 12px; min-width: 100px; overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: hidden; padding-bottom: 3px !important; padding-left: 3px !important; padding-right: 3px !important; padding-top: 3px !important; text-align: center; width: 222px;"><a class="image" href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/File:Round_%27em_up.jpg" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;"><img alt="" class="thumbimage" height="148" src="http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Round_%27em_up.jpg/220px-Round_%27em_up.jpg" style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: solid; border-bottom-width: 1px; border-color: initial; border-left-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-left-style: solid; border-left-width: 1px; border-right-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-right-style: solid; border-right-width: 1px; border-top-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-top-style: solid; border-top-width: 1px; border-width: initial; vertical-align: middle;" width="220" /></a><div class="thumbcaption" style="border-bottom-style: none; border-color: initial; border-left-style: none; border-right-style: none; border-top-style: none; border-width: initial; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.4em; padding-bottom: 3px !important; padding-left: 3px !important; padding-right: 3px !important; padding-top: 3px !important; text-align: left;"><div class="magnify" style="background-attachment: initial !important; background-clip: initial !important; background-color: initial !important; background-image: none !important; background-origin: initial !important; background-position: initial initial !important; background-repeat: initial initial !important; border-bottom-style: none !important; border-color: initial !important; border-left-style: none !important; border-right-style: none !important; border-top-style: none !important; border-width: initial !important; float: right;"><a class="internal" href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/File:Round_%27em_up.jpg" style="background-attachment: initial !important; background-clip: initial !important; background-color: initial !important; background-image: none !important; background-origin: initial !important; background-position: initial initial !important; background-repeat: initial initial !important; border-bottom-style: none !important; border-color: initial !important; border-left-style: none !important; border-right-style: none !important; border-top-style: none !important; border-width: initial !important; color: #002bb8; display: block; text-decoration: none;" title="Enlarge"><img alt="" height="11" src="http://bits.wikimedia.org/skins-1.5/common/images/magnify-clip.png" style="background-attachment: initial !important; background-clip: initial !important; background-color: white; background-image: none !important; background-origin: initial !important; background-position: initial initial !important; background-repeat: initial initial !important; border-bottom-style: none !important; border-color: initial !important; border-color: initial; border-left-style: none !important; border-right-style: none !important; border-top-style: none !important; border-width: initial !important; border-width: initial; display: block; vertical-align: middle;" width="15" /></a></div>Escaped sheep being led back to pasture with the enticement of food. This method of moving sheep works best with smaller flocks.</div></div></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><b>Flock dynamics in sheep are, as a rule, only exhibited in a group of four or more sheep.</b> <b>Fewer sheep may not react as normally expected when alone or with few other sheep</b>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-7" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>6<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;<b>For sheep, the primary defense mechanism is simply to flee from danger when their&nbsp;</b><a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Flight_zone" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Flight zone"><b>flight zone</b></a><b>&nbsp;is crossed. Secondly, cornered sheep may charge or threaten to do so through hoof stamping and aggressive posture. This is particularly true for ewes with newborn lambs.</b><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-8" style="font-style: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span><b>[</b></span><b>6</b><span><b>]</b></span></a></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;">In displaying flocking, <b>sheep have a strong lead-follow tendency</b>, and a leader often as not is simply the first sheep to move. However, <b>sheep do establish a&nbsp;</b><a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Pecking_order" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Pecking order"><b>pecking order</b></a><b>&nbsp;</b>through physical displays of dominance. Dominant animals are inclined to be more aggressive with other sheep, and usually feed first at&nbsp;<a class="extiw" href="http://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/trough" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #3366bb; text-decoration: none;" title="wiktionary:trough">troughs</a>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-38" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-38" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>39<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Primarily among rams, horn size is a factor in the flock hierarchy.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Budiansky78_39-0" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-Budiansky78-39" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>40<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Rams with different size horns may be less inclined to fight to establish pecking order, while rams with similarly sized horns are more so.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Budiansky78_39-1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-Budiansky78-39" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>40<span>]</span></a></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;">Sheep can become stressed when separated from their flock members.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-storey_6-6" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-storey-6" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>7<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Sheep can recognize individual human and ovine faces, and remember them for years.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-nature_40-0" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-nature-40" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>41<span>]</span></a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-minds_41-0" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-minds-41" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>42<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;<b>Relationships in flocks tend to be closest among related sheep: in mixed-breed flocks same-breed subgroups tend to form</b>, and a ewe and her direct descendants often move as a unit within large flocks.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-9" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>6<span>]</span></a></sup></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;">Sheep are frequently thought of as extremely unintelligent animals.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-42" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-42" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>43<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;A sheep's&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Herd_mentality" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Herd mentality">herd mentality</a>&nbsp;and quickness to flee and panic in the face of stress often make shepherding a difficult endeavor for the uninitiated. Despite these perceptions, a&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/University_of_Illinois_system" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="University of Illinois system">University of Illinois</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Monograph" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Monograph">monograph</a>&nbsp;on sheep found them to be just below pigs and on par with cattle in&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Intelligence_quotient" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Intelligence quotient">IQ</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-10" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>6<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;and some sheep have shown problem-solving abilities; a flock in&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/West_Yorkshire" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="West Yorkshire">West Yorkshire</a>,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/England" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="England">England</a>&nbsp;allegedly found a way to get over&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Cattle_grid" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Cattle grid">cattle grids</a>&nbsp;by rolling on their backs, although documentation of this has relied on anecdotal accounts.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-43" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-43" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>44<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;In addition to long-term facial recognition of individuals, sheep can also differentiate emotional states through facial characteristics.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-nature_40-1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-nature-40" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>41<span>]</span></a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-minds_41-1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-minds-41" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>42<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;If worked with patiently, sheep may learn their names, and many sheep are trained to be led by&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Halter" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Halter">halter</a>&nbsp;for showing and other purposes.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-11" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>6<span>]</span></a></sup>Sheep have also responded well to&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Clicker_training" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Clicker training">clicker training</a>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-12" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;"><span>[</span>6<span>]</span></a></sup>&nbsp;Very rarely, sheep are used as pack animals.&nbsp;<a href="http://www.blogger.com/wiki/Tibetan_people" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none;" title="Tibetan people">Tibetan</a>&nbsp;nomads distribute baggage equally throughout a flock as it is herded between living sites.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><span><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">[</a></span><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">6</a><span><a href="http://www.blogger.com/post-create.g?blogID=1992799306809150069#cite_note-hobby-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #002bb8; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">]</a></span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;">Sheep:&nbsp;<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><a href="http://www.teara.govt.nz/en/rural-language/7/1">http://www.teara.govt.nz/en/rural-language/7/1</a></span></span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/shrek-the-hermit-sheep-4e4b43bb46f2.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/shrek-the-hermit-sheep-1e8d172f6482.jpg" /></a></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><a href="http://www.teara.govt.nz/en/rural-language/7/1">Shrek</a></span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;">There was a sheep who avoided being&nbsp;</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;">sheered for over 6 years.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;">They called him&nbsp;Shrek&nbsp;the sheep.</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/shrek-the-hermit-sheep2-781345120dc8.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/shrek-the-hermit-sheep2-befec6a3e082.jpg" /></a><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><a href="http://www.teara.govt.nz/en/sheep-farming/5/4">Shrek</a></span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;">They finally caught up with him and sold&nbsp;</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;">enough wool for 20 mens suits!</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><br />
</span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"></span></span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: medium; line-height: 10px;"><a href="http://www.sheep101.info/sheepbible.html">The Lord is my shepherd</a></span></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: verdana, arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal;"><b>"A close relationship</b><br />
<i>My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me</i>. (John 10:27)<br />
The Bible describes close relationships between shepherds and their flocks. The sheep recognize the voice of the shepherd. They follow him (or her). The shepherd protects his flock and would give his life for them.<br />
It is known that animals can instantly recognize the voice of a familiar trusted person. Sheep have excellent memories for faces. They remember their handler. They also remember people who inflict abuse upon them.<br />
<b>Break a leg?</b><br />
There is a commonly-held belief that shepherds in Bible times would break the legs of lambs that constantly wandered away. According to the "story," the shepherd would then set the broken leg and carry the lamb on his shoulders until the leg healed, after which time the lamb would remain by the shepherd's side for the rest of its life. There is no such story in the Bible. To do so would be cruel and impractical."</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: verdana, arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: verdana, arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal;"><br />
</span></sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div><div style="line-height: 1.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0.4em;"><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-hobby_5-13" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1em;"><br />
</sup></div></span></div>
